message:
feat(index,core,durability): harden index open path with env-driven warm-up, stale WAL cleanup, and infrastructure improvements

This commit addresses several reliability and usability gaps across the
index, core, durability, and lexical crates:

**frankensearch-index:**

- Integrate env-configurable warm-up (`WarmUpConfig::from_env()`) into
  `VectorIndex::open()`, touching mmap pages before first search to reduce
  cold-start latency on systems with aggressive page eviction.
  Supports `FRANKENSEARCH_WARMUP_STRATEGY`, `_MAX_BYTES`,
  `_PARALLEL_READERS`, `_MIN_HEAT`, and `_HEAT_DECAY` env vars.

- Harden stale WAL handling: detect generation mismatches *before*
  truncation logic and proactively remove the stale WAL file instead of
  only clearing the in-memory entries. This prevents future opens from
  re-reading and re-discarding the same stale data.

- Fix WAL `append_wal_batch` TOCTOU race: use `.create(true).truncate(false)`
  on the fallback open path so a concurrent compaction deleting the WAL
  between the `create_new` check and the open does not cause an error.

- Add `search_fast_with_params()` to `TwoTierIndex` for brute-force
  parallelism overrides (threshold, chunk size, enable/disable). ANN
  retrieval is unaffected. Includes tests for default-match and
  sequential-override modes.

- Resolve ANN hit indices back to canonical VectorIndex positions after
  soft-delete filtering, so downstream consumers (quality scoring,
  explain metadata) receive valid indices instead of compact HNSW d_ids.

- Add `fast_vector_for_doc_id()`, `quality_vector_for_doc_id()`, and
  `semantic_vector_for_doc_id()` to `TwoTierIndex` for PRF feedback
  and MMR diversity reranking in the fusion crate.

- Clean up `rewrite_index()` signature: take `&[MergeSource]` instead of
  `&mut [MergeSource]` since the slice is not mutated. Fix integer
  overflow checks to use typed `u32`/`u64` intermediaries instead of
  raw `as` casts.

- Sort `mod` declarations alphabetically and add `clippy::too_many_lines`
  allow on `VectorIndex::open`.

**frankensearch-core:**

- Fix `QueryClass::classify` for multi-hyphenated issue/ticket IDs
  (e.g. `my-project-123`). Use `rsplitn` to split on the *last* hyphen
  so the prefix may itself contain hyphens or underscores. Add tests.

- Add `semantic_index` and `index` fields to test struct constructors
  throughout `types.rs` tests to match the struct definitions added in
  the previous semantic_index tracking commit.

**frankensearch-durability:**

- Implement `DefaultSymbolCodec`, an in-process symbol codec that
  satisfies the `SymbolCodec` trait without external runtime
  initialization. Provides trivial source-symbol chunking and XOR-free
  repair duplication so that durability wiring can produce valid
  encode/decode round-trips in tests and CLI tools.

- Re-export `DefaultSymbolCodec` from the crate root.

**frankensearch-lexical:**

- Expose `TantivyIndex::index_handle()` returning a clone of the inner
  `tantivy::Index`, used by the durability layer to protect/verify
  segment artifacts outside the lexical crate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
